status: empty_sleeve · the cart exists after the auction beat
notes: Astro-Cortex's last stick before folding (1989). Written by J.F. in the two years after A. Rocha left for Portland. Fewer than 900 copies pressed. Olaf never found one. On the shelf it exists as his hand-labeled empty sleeve · the catalog's one hole. In 2048, Tem finds one at auction for more than the cabin is worth.

shelf.shelf_condition: an empty sleeve, hand-labeled in Olaf's writing · the only stick rendered as a hole
shelf.provenance_note: Olaf's label reads, in full: 'BASILICA OF WIRES · Astro-Cortex 1987 · still looking · 30 yrs.' He kept the sleeve where the cart would go, so the shelf would know what it was missing.
shelf.cover_blurb: (from the 1987 catalog page, photocopied and folded into the sleeve) · A transmitter the size of a cathedral. A dial instead of a sword. Nine levels down, something is still broadcasting. Tune carefully.
back_of_case: · First-person wireframe · the mountain is one instrument.
· The tuner is the verb. Coarse and fine.
· Rooms exist per band. Learn the building's chord chart.
· The map is the health bar.
· Nine levels. One ending. Climb back out.
shelf.in_fiction_dev_note: J.F.'s game · the Earthman senior engineer, alone at the company after A. Rocha left for Portland in 1986. The whole game is mixed from sine waves and hum. At exactly 220 Hz the corridors hold still. The game never says why. Two people knew why.
shelf.meta_note: The eight-pointed star is etched above the master breaker, uncommented, in a game with no occult story at all. Wrongness by placement.

lore_tokens_conditional.basilica_message_room_reached: the teletype page, level nine, held at 220 · consumed by the Earthman Codex endpaper
lore_tokens_conditional.basilica_cart_acquired: the Vol 7 present-day auction beat · Tem bought it · the hole is filled
